title: Mean, Median, and Mode
concept: Mean, median, and mode are three ways to measure the middle of data, each best suited for different types of distributions.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is optimization in machine learning?

options.text: Adjusting a model's settings to minimize its prediction errors
Collecting more data to make the computer run faster
Deleting old files to save hard drive space

exercises.explain: Optimization is the process of adjusting a model's settings (like weights) to minimize its errors, using calculus to find the best settings.

cards.title: Startup salaries
cards.body: Imagine you're looking at salaries at a startup: 4 employees earn $50k each, but the CEO earns $1 million. If someone tells you the 'average' salary is $240k, is that helpful?

cards.title: Mean, Median, and Mode
cards.body: We measure the 'middle' of data using three metrics: mean (the arithmetic average), median (the exact middle value when sorted), and mode (the most frequent value).

cards.title: The scale, the line, and the crowd
cards.body: Think of the mean as a balancing point on a seesaw (highly sensitive to heavy weights), the median as the person standing in the exact middle of a single-file line, and the mode as the most popular shirt color in a crowd.

cards.title: When to use which
cards.body: If data has extreme outliers (like startup salaries), use the median to avoid a distorted middle. If you want to know the most common rating, use the mode. For symmetric data with no outliers, use the mean.

cards.title: The 'average' can lie
cards.body: People often use the word 'average' to only mean the mean. But if you have outliers, the mean will paint a misleading picture — the median is often the truer center.

cards.title: Mean vs. Median
cards.body: How they handle skewed data (like salaries):

exercises.prompt: Which measure of center is most heavily affected by a single extreme outlier (like a billionaire moving into a small town)?
exercises.explain: The mean sums all values and divides, meaning one massive value will drag the entire average up. The median simply looks at the middle value, remaining unchanged.

exercises.prompt: True or false: The median of the numbers [2, 3, 100] is 3.
exercises.explain: True! Sorting the numbers gives [2, 3, 100]. The exact middle number in this sorted list is 3.

exercises.sentence: If you want to find the most common color of cars sold this month, you should calculate the ___.
exercises.explain: The mode is the most frequently occurring value in a dataset, which is perfect for non-numerical categories like car colors.

exercises.prompt: Which dataset has a mean that is much higher than its median?
exercises.explain: The massive outlier (1000) drags the mean way up to 210.2, but the median (middle number) remains a tiny 14!

exercises.prompt: You're building an ML model to predict house prices. A few multi-million dollar mansions are skewing the average price upward, making your model's predictions look poor for normal homes. How should you define your target 'center' price?

options.text: Use the median price, as it ignores the high-end mansion outliers.
Use the mean price, because more data is always better.
Use the mode, because it's the easiest to calculate.

exercises.explain: Spot on! The median is robust to outliers and represents the typical home price much better. Nice job completing your first statistics lesson!
